checks.a_analysis: The output follows the structure and content of the source document accurately. All section headings and body text correspond to content found in the PDF. There are no invented sections or fabricated narratives.

Upon auditing the list of phone numbers on page 3 of the source document, there is a mismatch for one of the entries. The source PDF clearly lists '910-564-4194' (confirmed by OCR and visual inspection despite the red watermark). The output incorrectly represents this number as '910-564-2194'. Changing a digit in a government contact number is a high-severity defect.
All pages (1-7) of the source PDF are represented in the output. No entire sections of the minutes or attendee/vote tables have been omitted.
The tables in the output (attendance, guest lists, and roll call votes) contain all data rows present in the source PDF. For example, the guest list table correctly captures all ~32 entries.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Sections such as the 'Executive Director Report' and 'Education Committee Report' preserve all sub-points and key details. No substantial paragraphs or specific data points (like the PSAP training program percentages) are missing.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The roll call vote table in section 4 contains two spelling errors for proper names. 'Struble, Earl' is misspelled as 'Sturble, Earl', and 'Martinette, Buddy' is misspelled as 'Marinette, Buddy'. These errors alter the identity of board members in official voting records.

checks.a_analysis: The 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE' table and its associated metadata are repeated in the output (Elements 35 and 49). However, this repetition exists in the source document itself, appearing on both page 4 and at the top of page 6. Therefore, the output faithfully represents the intentional (albeit unusual) repetition in the source.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent and skips levels. Section 1 starts with H1 (Element 1), but Section 4 introduces an H3 (Element 36) for item 6 without an intervening H2. Furthermore, the heading application is inconsistent: item 6 is rendered as a heading (H3), while items 1-5, 7, 8, and 9-13 are rendered as standard paragraphs (Elements 14, 15, 17, 18, 19, 42, 43, 50, 52, 53, 55, 59, 61), even though they serve the same structural role in the source document.

The tables in the document, including the complex multi-section attendance table on page 1 and the roll call vote tables, are correctly mapped in the JSON. Headers and data rows are identified properly, and the 3-column structure of the attendance list is preserved.
The reading order in the output correctly follows the logical flow of the PDF from page 1 to page 7.

The list structure for item 5 is broken. While sub-items 5a and 5b are grouped in a list (Element 20), sub-items 5c, 5d, and 5e are converted into standalone paragraphs (Elements 24, 27, 30), losing their relationship to item 5. Item 5f is then placed in a new, separate list (Element 33). Additionally, the sub-sub-items under 5f ('i. Monthly Reports...' and 'ii. Mitchell County...') present in the source are entirely omitted from the output elements.
checks.b_evidence_source: f) Grant Project Updates per Reports\n i. Monthly Reports per PSP\n ii. Mitchell County Grant Extension Request

checks.a_analysis: There are no functional hyperlinks visible in the source document, and no fabricated links were introduced in the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The multi-column attendance tables on page 1 and the guest list on page 3 are preserved as structured tables. The numbered report sections (1-13) follow the same linear flow as the original document. The roll call and vote tables are correctly placed between the relevant text sections. Responsive layout changes, such as font style and margins, do not impact the logical organization of the information.

All tables from the source PDF are rendered correctly in the HTML. This includes the complex multi-column attendance tables on page 1, the roll call tables on pages 2 and 3 with checkmarks, and the vote tables on pages 4 and 6. The columns are properly aligned, headers are visually distinct, and borders are used to separate cells, ensuring readability and understanding of the data.

Meaningful images, specifically the NC DIT logos within the roll call tables, are successfully rendered in the HTML output. The diagonal red watermark ('APPROVED BY NC911 BOARD 06/26/2020') present on the PDF pages is correctly omitted, as it is a decorative element and its removal is an intentional accessibility improvement.

There is no visual duplication of body content introduced by the conversion process. While the 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE' table appears twice in the HTML, this is a faithful representation of the source PDF where the same table is printed on both page 4 and page 6. The presence of headings in the Table of Contents that repeat in the body is standard accessible design and does not constitute content duplication.
